name: Agentic AI
description: How AI agents collaborate within the mesh, using MeshPlugin tools and integrating with external AI services

content.title: Agentic AI Architecture
content.logo: data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg viewBox='0 0 48 48' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%3E%3Cdefs%3E%3ClinearGradient id='ai' x1='0%25' y1='0%25' x2='100%25' y2='100%25'%3E%3Cstop offset='0%25' stop-color='%23ff6f00'/%3E%3Cstop offset='100%25' stop-color='%23e65100'/%3E%3C/linearGradient%3E%3C/defs%3E%3Crect width='48' height='48' rx='8' fill='url(%23ai)'/%3E%3Ccircle cx='24" cy='18' r='8' fill='white'/%3E%3Cpath d='M12 38c0-6.6 5.4-12 12-12s12 5.4 12 12' fill='white'/%3E%3Ccircle cx='24' cy='18' r='4' fill='%23ff6f00'/%3E%3C/svg%3E
content.content:
# Agentic AI Architecture MeshWeaver integrates AI agents as first-class citizens in the mesh. Agents can query data, navigate structures, execute tasks, and collaborate with other agents - all through unified mesh references. ## Design Philosophy ### Minimal System Prompts Unlike traditional approaches with extensive system prompts, MeshWeaver agents have **lean prompts**. Instead of encoding all knowledge upfront, agents: 1. Query the mesh for task descriptions 2. Look up data schemas dynamically 3. Discover available operations at runtime 4. Read documentation stored as mesh nodes This keeps agents adaptable and reduces prompt maintenance. ### Agents as Data Elements Agents are stored as nodes in the mesh hierarchy: ``` ACME/ ProductLaunch/ Agent/ <- Agents for this project Researcher Planner Todo/ ... ``` When a task is invoked, MeshWeaver selects the **lowest-most agent** in the hierarchy. This allows: - Problem-specific agents at lower levels - Generic fallback agents at higher levels - Override behavior without changing code ## Multi-Agent Collaboration ```mermaid flowchart TB U[User Request] --> P[Planner Agent
Flagship Model] P -->|Tasks| R[Researcher Agent
Small LM] P -->|Execute| E[Executor Agent] R -->|Context| P R -->|Data| E E -->|Results| P subgraph Mesh["Mesh Graph"] T[Task Descriptions] S[Schemas] D[Data] end R <-->|Query| Mesh E <-->|CRUD| Mesh ``` ### Agent Roles | Agent | Model Size | Purpose | |-------|------------|----------| | **Planner** | Flagship (GPT-4, Claude) | Decomposes tasks, orchestrates workflow | | **Researcher** | Small LM (GPT-3.5, Haiku) | Gathers context, searches data | | **Executor** | Medium | Performs CRUD operations, runs workflows | ## MeshPlugin Tools Agents interact with the mesh through `MeshPlugin`, which provides these operations: ```mermaid flowchart LR subgraph Tools["MeshPlugin"] G[Get] S[Search] GD[GetDocument] N[NavigateTo] U[Update] D[Delete] end A[AI Agent] --> Tools Tools --> M[Mesh] ``` ### Read Operations **Get** - Retrieve data by path: ``` Get("@graph/org1") -> Returns node JSON Get("@graph/*") -> Returns children Get("@pricing/MS-2024") -> Returns pricing data ``` **Search** - Query with GitHub-style syntax: ``` Search("nodeType:Organization") -> All organizations Search("name:*acme*") -> Name contains 'acme' Search("status:active", "@graph") -> Active items under graph ``` **GetDocument** - Fetch markdown content: ``` GetDocument("MeshWeaver/QuerySyntax") -> Documentation content ``` ### Write Operations **Update** - Create or modify nodes: ``` Update("@graph/neworg", {"name": "New Org", "nodeType": "Organization"}) ``` **Delete** - Remove nodes: ``` Delete("@graph/oldorg", recursive=true) ``` ### Navigation **NavigateTo** - Display a node's view in the UI: ``` NavigateTo("@graph/org1") -> Shows org1's detail view ``` ## Path Shorthand The `@` prefix provides convenient shorthand: | Shorthand | Full Path | |-----------|----------| | `@graph/org1` | `graph/org1` | | `@NodeType/*` | `NodeType/*` | | `@pricing/MS-2024` | `pricing/MS-2024` | ## External AI Integration ### MCP Protocol MeshWeaver supports the **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** for bidirectional AI integration: ```mermaid flowchart LR subgraph External SF[Snowflake AI] OT[Other Tools] end subgraph MeshWeaver MW[MeshWeaver AI] end External <-->|MCP Protocol| MeshWeaver ``` **Consuming External Tools:** - Include Snowflake MCP server to access Cortex AI - Add any MCP-compatible tool provider - Tools appear automatically in agent context **Exposing MeshWeaver:** - MeshWeaver can expose its mesh operations via MCP - External AI systems can query and modify mesh data - Enables integration with enterprise AI platforms ### Snowflake Integration Access Snowflake AI through: 1. **SQL**: Execute Cortex functions via data sources 2. **MCP**: Direct integration with Snowflake's MCP server ```sql -- Example: Sentiment analysis via Cortex SELECT SNOWFLAKE.CORTEX.SENTIMENT(comment) FROM reviews ``` ## Agent Context Discovery Agents discover their context from the mesh: 1. **Task Descriptions**: Stored as markdown nodes 2. **Data Schemas**: NodeType definitions with field metadata 3. **Available Operations**: Registered handlers and workflows 4. **Domain Knowledge**: Documentation throughout the hierarchy Example agent bootstrap: ``` 1. Agent receives task: "Create Q4 report" 2. Agent queries: Search("nodeType:TaskDescription Q4 report") 3. Agent reads: GetDocument("ACME/Reports/Q4Template") 4. Agent discovers: Get("@ACME/Reports/Schema") 5. Agent executes with full context ``` ## Benefits 1. **Adaptability**: Agents learn from mesh, not hardcoded prompts 2. **Hierarchy**: Override agents at any level 3. **Collaboration**: Multiple agents with specialized roles 4. **Integration**: MCP connects any AI system 5. **Transparency**: All agent actions are mesh operations
